title: C3.ai Stock Just Hit a New 52-Week Low. Should You Buy the Dip?
content:
C3.ai (AI) shares slipped 6% on Monday amid investor anxiety ahead of the company’s quarterly earnings scheduled for Wednesday, Feb. 25. Consensus is for this enterprise software firm to lose $0.73 per share in its fiscal Q3, which would mean a nearly 18% decline versus the same quarter last year.

Ahead of the release, AI stock is trading at a 52-week low of $9.79, with a 14-day relative strength index (RSI) at roughly 34, indicating bearish momentum may soon approach exhaustion.

More News from Barchart

Why Citi Analysts Think You Should Buy Microsoft Stock Now Netflix Investors Breathe Sigh of Relief: $83 Billion WBD Deal In DOJ Crosshairs Stanley Druckenmiller Just Exited Sandisk Stock. Should You Buy SNDK After Its Blowout Earnings? Get exclusive insights with the FREE Barchart Brief newsletter. Subscribe now for quick, incisive midday market analysis you won't find anywhere else.www.barchart.com

Is C3.ai Stock a Bargain Right Now?

Investors are cautioned against buying the dip in AI shares, as the current market environment presents substantial headwinds for enterprise software firms. 
Sentiment has curdled into a “SaaS-pocalypse” amid investor fears that artificial intelligence (AI) could disrupt legacy software providers like C3.ai.

And this internal pressure is now colliding with a volatile macro landscape, including the recent Supreme Court ruling striking down President Donald Trump's emergency tariffs.

These headwinds threaten to inflate operational costs and freeze enterprise spending, which could prove detrimental for a business like C3.ai that’s already grappling with a 20% revenue decline.

C3.ai Shares Remain Expensive To Own

C3.ai stock remains unattractive because the departure of its founder and chief executive, Thomas Siebel, has left a vacuum that new CEO Stephen Ehikian must fill amid a securities class action lawsuit.

Investors are advised to take this transition seriously, as Siebel’s “high-touch” sales style was central to the company’s enterprise deal-making.

Moreover, the AI boom has curiously bypassed C3.ai’s financials; it’s path to profitability remains a puzzle with no clear solution in sight. 
In fact, a lack of visibility even forced its management to withdraw its full-year guidance in Sept.

All in all, a persistent cash burn, execution missteps, and a price-to-sales (P/S) multiple that sits at about 4x despite shrinking growth, this artificial intelligence stock looks expensive compared to its profitable peers.

How Wall Street Recommends Playing C3.ai

Wall Street analysts also seem to believe that C3.ai is a falling knife that carries far more risk than reward in 2026. 
The consensus rating on AI shares sits at a “Hold,” with price targets as low as $8, signaling potential downside of another 20% from here.

title: C3.ai (AI) Reports Earnings Tomorrow: What To Expect
content:
Enterprise AI software company C3.ai (NYSE:AI) will be reporting results this Wednesday afternoon. Here’s what investors should know.

C3.ai met analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $75.15 million, down 20.3% year on year. It was a strong quarter for the company, with a solid beat of analysts’ EBITDA estimates and an impressive beat of analysts’ billings estimates.

Is C3.ai a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting C3.ai’s revenue to decline 23.4% year on year, a reversal from the 26% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.C3.ai Total Revenue

Analysts covering the company have generally reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting they anticipate the business to stay the course heading into earnings. C3.ai rarely misses Wall Street’s revenue estimates.

Looking at C3.ai’s peers in the data and analytics software segment, some have already reported their Q4 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. Teradata delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 2.9%, beating analysts’ expectations by 5.4%, and Confluent reported revenues up 20.5%, topping estimates by 2.2%. Teradata traded up 29.6% following the results while Confluent’s stock price was unchanged.

Read our full analysis of Teradata’s results here and Confluent’s results here.

The outlook for 2025 remains clouded by potential trade policy changes and corporate tax discussions, which could impact business confidence and growth. Unfortunately, data and analytics software stocks have struggled in this environment as share prices are down 22% on average over the last month. C3.ai is down 24.3% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $14.13 (compared to the current share price of $9.78).

Today’s young investors likely haven’t read the timeless lessons in Gorilla Game: Picking Winners In High Technology because it was written more than 20 years ago when Microsoft and Apple were first establishing their supremacy. But if we apply the same principles, then enterprise software stocks leveraging their own generative AI capabilities may well be the Gorillas of the future. So, in that spirit, we are excited to present our Special Free Report on a profitable, fast-growing enterprise software stock that is already riding the automation wave and looking to catch the generative AI next.

title: Here's What Investors Must Expect Ahead of C3.ai's Q3 Earnings
content:
C3.ai, Inc. AI is scheduled to report its third-quarter fiscal 2026 (ended Jan. 31, 2026) results on Feb. 25, after the closing bell.

In the last reported quarter, the company’s adjusted loss per share of 25 cents contracted from the Zacks Consensus Estimate of a loss per share of 32 cents but widened year over year from an adjusted loss per share of six cents. Revenues of $75.1 million remained almost flat with the consensus mark but declined year over year by 20.4%.

AI’s earnings surpassed estimates in each of the trailing four quarters, with an average surprise of 24.1%.

How are Estimates Placed for AI Stock?

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for the fiscal third quarter indicates a loss per share of 29 cents, which has remained unchanged over the past 60 days. The estimate is 141.7% wider than the loss per share of 12 cents reported in the year-ago quarter.

The consensus estimate for revenues is pegged at $75.8 million, indicating a 23.2% year-over-year decline from $98.8 million.

C3.ai, Inc. Price and EPS SurpriseC3.ai, Inc. Price and EPS Surprise

C3.ai, Inc. price-eps-surprise | C3.ai, Inc. Quote

Factors Likely to Have Shaped C3.ai’s Q3 Performance

Revenues

During the fiscal third quarter, the company’s top-line performance is expected to have declined year over year because of lower contributions from Subscription (contributed 93% to the second quarter fiscal 2026 total revenues) and Professional services (contributed 7% to the second quarter fiscal 2026 total revenues) operations.

The downturn is likely to have been caused by weaker recognized revenues despite ongoing deal activity, because of a higher mix of Initial Production Deployments (IPDs), as many deals were still in early implementation phases. Also, a decline in prioritized engineering services and the number of service, consulting and training projects for C3 AI Platform and C3 AI Application customers is expected to have hit the performance growth.

For the fiscal third quarter, C3.ai expects total revenues to be between $72 million and $80 million.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for revenues from the Subscription and Professional services operations is pegged at $68 million and $7.5 million, reflecting year-over-year declines of 20.9% and 42.7%, respectively.

Margins

AI’s bottom line is expected to have plunged in the fiscal third quarter, mainly due to a decline in prioritized engineering service projects, along with higher payroll and contractor costs.

Besides, the company expects to witness moderated gross margins in the near term because of a high mix of IPDs, which carry a greater cost of revenues during the initial production deployment phase, and due to the investments in expanding its support capacity and lower economies of scale.

The Zacks model expects gross margins for the Subscription and Professional services operations to be 49.9% and 74%, respectively. The estimates indicate year-over-year contractions of 610 basis points (bps) and 600 bps, respectively.

Leer más

What the Zacks Model Says for C3.ai

Our proven model does not conclusively predict an earnings beat for C3.ai this time around. The combination of a positive Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), 2 (Buy) or 3 (Hold) increases the odds of an earnings beat. This is not the case here, as you will see below.

AI’s Earnings ESP: The company has an Earnings ESP of -9.40%. You can uncover the best stocks before they’re reported with our Earnings ESP Filter.

AI’s Zacks Rank: It currently carries a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell).

Stocks Poised to Beat Earnings Estimates

Here are some stocks from the Zacks Computer and Technology sector, which according to our model, have the right combination of elements to post an earnings beat.

Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd CRDO has an Earnings ESP of +3.54% and a Zacks Rank of 1 at present. 

Credo Technology’s earnings beat estimates in each of the last four quarters, the average surprise being 38.5%. The company’s earnings for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 are expected to surge 284% year over year.

Sportradar Group AG SRAD currently has an Earnings ESP of +6.06% and a Zacks Rank of 3.

Sportradar’s earnings topped estimates in two of the last four quarters and missed on the remaining two occasions, the average surprise being 61.3%.  The company’s earnings for the fourth quarter of 2025 are expected to be nine cents per share compared with break-even earnings in the year-ago period.

StoneCo Ltd. STNE has an Earnings ESP of +5.52% and a Zacks Rank of 3.

StoneCo’s earnings beat estimates in three of the last four quarters and met on the remaining occasion, the average surprise being 9.1%. The company’s earnings for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 are expected to rise 23.1% year over year.

title: Buy Palantir on Recent Softness Amid Massive Short-Term Price Upside
content:
Artificial intelligence (AI)-powered data mining and analytics company Palantir Technologies Inc. PLTR has been witnessing a price downturn in the past six months despite reporting strong earnings results and operational performances.

PLTR is currently trading at a discount of 34.8% from its all-time high recorded on Nov. 3. This weakness in the stock price opened up a lucrative opportunity for investors to enter this AI analytics software giant, as it has massive short-term price upside potential.

The chart below shows the price performance of PLTR year to date.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Attractive Diversified Business Model

In the last reported quarter, government revenues climbed 60.4% year over year to $730 million. Commercial revenues jumped 81.8% annually to reach $677 million. As of Dec. 31, the total customer count was 954.

Palantir’s core customer base comprises businesses seeking tailored AI/ML services, particularly large government and corporate clients willing to invest heavily in its systems. This has generated solid revenues, registering a 21.3% CAGR from 2020 to 2024.

PLTR’s commercial business has gathered pace besides its traditional government contracts. This is primarily due to PLTR’s aggressive venture in the AI space. In 2023, PLTR launched its Artificial Intelligence Platform (“AIP”), an AI-powered system that helps customers quickly concentrate and analyze data and discover how it can help advance their business goals.

AIP provides unified access to open-source, self-hosted, and commercial large language models (LLMs) that can transform structured and unstructured data into LLM-understandable objects and turn organizations' actions and processes into tools for humans and LLM-driven agents. This shift in revenue structure has enabled the company to no longer depend on government defense agencies.

Extensive Thrust on AI

Palantir’s AI strategy is comprehensive, combining its proprietary Foundry and Gotham platforms with a solid plan to promote AI adoption across both government and commercial sectors. Its AIP is the backbone of these capabilities, enabling organizations to process large datasets and derive real-time insights.

This is especially valuable in sectors requiring extensive data integration, such as defense, healthcare, finance and intelligence, where operational efficiency and decision-making speed are critical.

In the government sector, PLTR is aligning its AI strategy with U.S. defense priorities. Its work in high-profile initiatives, such as the Department of Defense’s Open DAGIR project, highlights its ability to modernize military operations through AI-driven solutions where data interoperability and real-time decision-making capabilities are imperative. These capabilities solidify Palantir’s position as a key player in the defense sector.

Story Continues

In the commercial space, PLTR’s AIP boot camps — providing hands-on experience to over 1,000 companies — have proven instrumental in customer acquisition. Boot camps showcase the platform’s capabilities and demonstrate its adaptability across logistics, manufacturing, and supply chain management.

Guidance Signals Confidence, Not Caution

Management’s outlook for 2026 reinforced the strength of the current trajectory. First-quarter revenues are guided to $1.53 billion at the midpoint, implying 8.5% sequential growth. For the full year, Palantir expects revenues of roughly $7.19 billion at the midpoint, indicating 61% year-over-year growth.

Profitability is expected to remain exceptional. Adjusted operating income guidance of around $4.13 billion implies further margin expansion, while adjusted free cash flow is projected between $3.9 billion and $4.1 billion. The company also expects to maintain a Rule of 40 score near 118%, a level rarely achieved at this scale.

Future Catalysts

Palantir plans to invest more in hiring talent and advancing product features using its AI-powered technologies. This will enable the company to win and properly execute both government and commercial contracts.

PLTR’s modular sales approach expands beyond the core customer base, allowing clients to purchase specific product components instead of committing to the full platform upfront. This model also incorporates usage-based pricing, which lowers the entry barrier for new clients.

By starting small, clients can gradually increase spending as they scale their usage of PLTR’s solutions. This has expanded its U.S. commercial customer base. Aside from Palantir Technologies, BigBear.ai Holdings Inc. BBAI and C3.ai Inc. AI are the other major contenders in the AI-powered defense analytics software segment.

Excellent Estimate Revisions

For 2026, the Zacks Consensus Estimate currently shows revenues of $7.22 billion, suggesting an improvement of 61.3% year over year and earnings per share of $1.34, indicating an increase of 78.7% year over year. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 28.8% over the last 30 days.

For 2027, the Zacks Consensus Estimate currently shows revenues of $10.11 billion, suggesting an improvement of 40% year over year and earnings per share of $1.89, indicating an increase of 40.7% year over year. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 36% over the last 30 days.

Moreover, PLTR has an impressive long-term growth potential. At present, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for PLTR’s earnings per share for long-term (3-5 years) has surged to 49.5% compared with just 16.3% growth for the broad-market index — the S&P 500.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Massive Short-Term Price Upside

The short-term average price target of brokerage firms for the stock represents an increase of 48.2% from the last closing price of $135.24. The brokerage target price is currently in the range of $260-$90. This indicates a maximum upside of 92.3% and a downside of 33.3%.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Investment Thesis

Palantir currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

PLTR is set to capitalize on the AI boom with robust business execution and an industry-leading product portfolio. At this stage, each and every fall in the stock price will open a good entry point.

title: Assessing C3.ai (AI) Valuation As Growth In Federal Bookings Contrasts With Revenue Contraction Guidance

Recent results at C3.ai (AI) highlight a mixed picture, with strong federal bookings and high recurring subscription revenue paired with guidance for revenue contraction and continued operating losses that has kept trading cautious.

See our latest analysis for C3.ai.

The cautious tone around revenue contraction and losses is showing up in the share price, with a 30 day share price return of an 18.2% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 63.6% decline, pointing to fading momentum despite product and partnership headlines such as the new Vonage backed C3 AI Field Services module.

Most Popular Narrative: 29.2% Undervalued

At a last close of $10.38 versus a narrative fair value of $14.67, the most widely followed view sees meaningful upside, but it rests on some punchy assumptions.

The rapid expansion of AI deployments across manufacturing, chemicals, defense, and government clients, demonstrated by fresh enterprise-wide commitments from Nucor, Qemetica, HII, and U.S. Army projects, signals accelerating enterprise adoption of advanced AI platforms, which is expected to drive strong, multi-year revenue growth as adoption moves from pilots to broad production rollouts.

However, you still need to weigh ongoing revenue contraction and large operating losses, as well as heavy reliance on partner led sales that could blunt those optimistic assumptions.

Another View: Price Tag Still Looks Full

That 29.2% “undervalued” narrative sits awkwardly next to the current P/S of 4.1x. This screens as expensive versus the US Software industry at 3.5x, peers at 2.3x, and a fair ratio of 2x that the market could move towards. This leaves less room for error if growth or profitability disappoints.

title: C3.ai Vonage Deal Tests Real World Payoff For Field Service AI
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

C3.ai (NYSE:AI) has partnered with Vonage to introduce an AI powered field services module focused on mobile field operations. The collaboration uses AI agents with real time voice and video to support technicians in the field. The new module aims to tackle fragmented information, skill gaps, and operational inefficiencies in enterprise field service teams. Vonage communications APIs and Quality on Demand tools are being integrated to support secure, verified, and compliant field interactions.

C3.ai comes into this update with a share price of $10.38 and multi year returns that have been under pressure, including a 63.6% decline over the past year and a 90.8% decline over five years. Moves like this collaboration highlight how the company is trying to sharpen its focus on practical enterprise use cases rather than broad AI narratives.

For you as an investor, the key question is whether embedding AI directly into field workflows can translate into stronger adoption from existing and new enterprise customers. The impact of this partnership will likely show up first in customer case studies and product traction metrics, long before it has any clear effect on NYSE:AI’s share price.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for C3.ai by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on C3.ai.NYSE:AI Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Feb 2026

0 things going right for C3.ai that this headline doesn't cover.

This collaboration pushes C3.ai further into hands-on use cases where AI directly supports technicians, not just back office analysts. By integrating its enterprise AI models with Vonage’s voice, video and network APIs, C3.ai is trying to make its platform more embedded in day to day operations such as maintenance, inspections and repairs. For you, the key angle is whether field service buyers see this as differentiated enough versus broader AI platforms from players like Microsoft, Amazon or ServiceNow that also target operational workflows. Given C3.ai’s partner led bookings and current revenue and profitability challenges, a focused module like this could help deepen relationships with existing industrial and government customers if it shows clear productivity and compliance benefits.

How This Fits Into The C3.ai Narrative

The launch of C3 AI Field Services directly supports the narrative that new programs and partnerships can broaden reach and accelerate enterprise adoption, especially in sectors with large distributed workforces. Execution risk is still a theme, so success here depends on C3.ai turning this collaboration into repeatable deployments rather than one off wins, which has been a concern in prior quarters. The narrative focuses heavily on hyperscaler and systems integrator channels, while this Vonage tie up adds a communications and network intelligence angle that is not fully captured in the existing story.

Story Continues

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for C3.ai to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ C3.ai remains unprofitable and is not forecast to reach profitability in the next 3 years, so additional product modules still sit on top of a loss making base. ⚠️ Analysts highlight ongoing revenue contraction guidance and execution issues, so there is a risk that new partnerships do not offset weaker core growth. 🎁 The focus on practical field service problems, such as first time fix rates and compliance, may help C3.ai defend its position against general purpose AI platforms from larger competitors. 🎁 If customers adopt these AI powered field workflows at scale, it could support stronger partner led bookings and deepen C3.ai’s role inside industrial and government accounts.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you might want to track whether C3.ai and Vonage start naming reference customers, particularly in industries like manufacturing, energy or defense where C3.ai already has a presence. Watch for management commentary on how often C3 AI Field Services is included in new deals or upsells, and whether it contributes meaningfully to bookings, especially as C3.ai faces guidance that points to revenue contraction and continued operating losses. Context from rivals such as Microsoft, Amazon and ServiceNow on their own field focused AI offerings can also help you judge how differentiated this module really is.

To stay informed on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for C3.ai, head to the community page for C3.ai to follow updates on the top community narratives.

title: Earnings week ahead: NVDA, CRM, HD, BIDU, LOW, DELL, SNOW, AMC, ZM, and more
content:
[earnings]
MCCAIG

The last week of February brings another packed stretch of earnings, with a mix of megacap technology leaders, consumer bellwethers, energy producers, financial firms, and high-growth disruptors set to report.

The lineup spans AI heavyweights, real estate income plays, cloud software providers, and cyclical industrial names, offering investors a broad read on enterprise spending, housing-linked demand, digital advertising, and commodity trends as the quarter progresses.

Technology and AI will dominate the spotlight with updates from NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), Snowflake (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]), The Trade Desk (TTD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TTD]), Zoom Communications (ZM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZM]), Zscaler (ZS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZS]), C3.ai [https://C3.ai] (AI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AI]), IonQ (IONQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IONQ]), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QBTS]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), HP (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]), Workday (WDAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDAY]), Intuit (INTU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTU]), CoreWeave (CRWV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWV]), and SoundHound AI (SOUN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOUN]).

Consumer, retail, and payments trends will be reflected through results from The Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]), Lowe's (LOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOW]), Block (XYZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XYZ]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), Baidu (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU]), Lucid Group (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]), AMC Entertainment (AMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMC]), Hims & Hers Health (HIMS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HIMS]), and Teladoc Health (TDOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDOC]).

Energy, infrastructure, and materials updates from ONEOK (OKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKE]), Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]), Cheniere Energy (LNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LNG]), Diamondback Energy (FANG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FANG]), EOG Resources (EOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EOG]), MP Materials (MP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MP]), and First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]) will offer signals on commodity demand, pricing, and capital discipline.

Real estate, income, and financially linked names, including Realty Income (O [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/O]), VICI Properties (VICI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VICI]), American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IIPR]), EPR Properties (EPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPR]), Main Street Capital (MAIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAIN]), and Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TD]), will add insight into financing conditions, leasing demand, and credit trends.

Altogether, the week’s reports should provide a wide-angle view on AI spending momentum, consumer resilience, capital investment, and interest-rate sensitivity as markets head toward the final stretch of the quarter.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of February 23 to February 27:

MONDAY, FEBRUARY 23

DOMINION ENERGY (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D])

Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]) is set to report Q4 earnings before Monday’s market open, with analysts expecting roughly a 16% Y/Y increase in profits on revenue growth of more than 9%.

The utility was recently in focus after securing a court ruling [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4539989-dominion-wins-court-ok-to-resume-virginia-offshore-wind-project-stopped-by-trump] allowing it to resume work on its $11.2B Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project. A U.S. District Court granted a preliminary injunction preventing the federal government from enforcing a stop-work order after the company argued the halt was causing irreparable financial harm. Dominion has already invested nearly $9B in the project, which is expected to generate enough power for about 600K homes and remains central to its long-term growth strategy.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating and sell-side consensus both stand at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D/ratings/quant-ratings].

However, SA Investing Group Leader The Value Portfolio rates the stock a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4867876-dominion-energy-coastal-wind-moving-forward-with-a-strong-portfolio], citing Dominion’s roughly $50B capital investment plan, a dividend yield near 4.3%, and a long-term EPS growth target of about 6%. With the offshore wind project now about two-thirds complete and data-center-driven electricity demand rising, the company expects stronger cash flow and continued shareholder returns, though project risks and potential equity dilution remain key watch points.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.67
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $2.23B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, missing revenue expectations in 5 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: ONEOK (OKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKE]), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IIPR]), Diamondback Energy (FANG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FANG]), Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBBY]), Ovintiv (OVV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OVV]), Domino's Pizza (DPZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DPZ]), BioMarin Pharmaceutical (BMRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMRN]), and more.

TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 24

HOME DEPOT (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD])

Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]) is set to report Q4 earnings before Tuesday’s market open, with analysts expecting roughly a 16% Y/Y decline in profits alongside a ~4% drop in revenue.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating on the stock, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant model remains at Hold, reflecting concerns around growth and valuation.

SA author Justin Purohit also takes a neutral stance [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872410-home-depot-q4-earnings-preview-housing-trends-in-focus-shares-fairly-valued] ahead of the release, noting that shares have held up relatively well despite softness in the housing market, with management’s outlook for housing demand through 2026 likely to be a key focus. He views the stock as fairly valued in the current environment.

By contrast, contributor Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA, retains a Sell rating [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4865053-home-depot-canary-in-the-coal-mine], arguing that recent weakness in the stock may only be the beginning as margins stay under pressure. He also highlights that valuation remains elevated relative to profitability, while macro risks tied to the housing cycle have intensified lately.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $38.03B
 	* Earnings Insight: Home Depot has consistently outperformed revenue estimates in the past 6 of the 8 quarters and EPS forecasts in 50% of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Realty Income (O [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/O]), Lucid Group (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]), AMC Entertainment Holdings (AMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMC]), American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), HP (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]), First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]), EOG Resources (EOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EOG]), Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BNS]), Workday (WDAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDAY]), Tanger Factory Outlet Centers (SKT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SKT]), Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLNE]), Mosaic (MOS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOS]), LTC Properties (LTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LTC]), Elanco Animal Health (ELAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ELAN]), Axon Enterprise (AXON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXON]), Range Resources (RRC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RRC]), DigitalOcean (DOCN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCN]), Amer Sports (AS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AS]), CAVA Group (CAVA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAVA]), and more.

WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 25

NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA])

NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) will headline the week’s earnings, with its Q4 report due Wednesday after the close. Analysts expect revenue and earnings to surge roughly 70% Y/Y, driven by sustained AI-chip demand and continued strength in data-center spending.

Recent developments have kept the company in focus, including a broad multi-year AI infrastructure partnership with Meta [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4552879-nvidia-announces-multi-year-partnership-with-meta-to-expand-ai-infrastructure] Platforms (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), which expands deployment of Nvidia’s Grace CPUs, Spectrum-X networking, GB300 systems, and confidential computing tools across Meta’s platforms.

Oppenheimer reiterated [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4553985-nvidia-likely-to-offer-typical-upside-when-it-reports-q4-results-oppenheimer] its Outperform rating and $265 price target on NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), saying the company could deliver a “typical” $2B–$3B revenue upside in Q4, driven by strong demand for GB300 Ultra systems and rising hyperscaler capex, which is projected to climb to about $650B in 2026 from over $400B in 2025.

Analyst Rick Schafer also pointed to Nvidia’s product roadmap as a key growth lever, with the higher-priced Vera Rubin platform expected to ramp later this year and potentially add around $8B in revenue, while expanding AI workloads, rapid growth in reasoning tokens, and a potential $50B opportunity in China could further lift Nvidia’s total addressable market, now estimated near $4T, reinforcing the view that the company remains the dominant platform in AI infrastructure.

Views on NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) remain sharply split heading into earnings.

While Wall Street broadly holds a Strong Buy stance, Seeking Alpha’s Quant model is more cautious with a Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA/ratings/quant-ratings] on valuation grounds.

Seeking Alpha contributor Dair Sansyzbayev is firmly bullish, calling Nvidia a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872717-nvidia-stock-deep-discount-before-earnings] given its dominance in AI accelerators, accelerating revenue and EPS growth, strong operating leverage, and powerful free-cash-flow generation, with hyperscaler spending expected to drive another blowout quarter.

Julia Ostian takes a more measured stance, rating the stock Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4871478-nvidia-q4-why-even-a-record-beat-could-sink-the-stock]. She acknowledges Nvidia’s unmatched ecosystem, margins, and leadership but warns that expectations for a massive beat are extremely high, with rising inventory and sentiment risk potentially triggering volatility, making post-earnings entry or dollar-cost averaging more prudent.

On the bearish side, Oriental Trader assigns a Strong Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4870585-nvidia-stock-may-be-at-risk-as-big-tech-capex-concerns-mount], arguing Nvidia’s valuation depends heavily on sustained Big Tech AI capex, and any slowdown could expose significant downside given its enormous market cap and still-elevated forward multiple.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $65.69B
 	* Earnings Insight: Nvidia outperformed revenue and EPS estimates in 8 consecutive quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), Zoom Communications (ZM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZM]), Teladoc Health (TDOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDOC]), Trade Desk (TTD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TTD]), Snowflake (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]), Lowe's Companies (LOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOW]), Amarin (AMRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMRN]), VICI Properties (VICI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VICI]), C3.ai (AI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AI]), Apache (APA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APA]), EPR Properties (EPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPR]), Starwood Property Trust (STWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STWD]), TJX Companies (TJX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TJX]), Agilent Technologies (A [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/A]), Bank of Montreal (BMO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMO]), ACADIA Pharmaceuticals (ACAD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACAD]), Magnite (MGNI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGNI]), Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IONS]), Nutanix (NTNX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTNX]), Geron (GERN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GERN]), Star Bulk Carriers (SBLK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SBLK]), Schrödinger (SDGR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SDGR]), Icahn Enterprises L.P. (IEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IEP]), Pure Storage (PSTG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSTG]), and more.

THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 26

BAIDU (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU])

Chinese search giant Baidu (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU]) is set to report Q4 results before Thursday’s open, with analysts expecting profit to decline roughly 47% Y/Y.

The company recently approved a new $5B share repurchase program [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4547623-baidu-announces-5b-buyback-and-dividend-policy] running through December 2028 and introduced a dividend policy that could include both regular and special payouts, signaling a more shareholder-return-focused approach.

While sell-side analysts retain a Buy rating, Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU/ratings/quant-ratings].

SA contributor Michael Ngan also rates the stock a Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4871246-baidus-ai-initiatives-are-exciting-what-about-search-and-the-rest], noting Baidu’s core search business continues to face structural pressure from stagnant user growth and declining ARPU. Management is increasingly leaning on AI initiatives to drive the next growth phase, with infrastructure, applications, and its Kunlunxin AI-chip unit gaining traction and potential IPO visibility. However, these newer businesses remain less profitable than search, creating a near-term margin headwind despite improving long-term positioning.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.41
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $4.73B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has topped EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, exceeding revenue expectations in 5 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Viatris (VTRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTRS]), Main Street Capital Holdings (MAIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAIN]), TD Bank Group (TD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TD]), Cronos Group (CRON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRON]), Zscaler (ZS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZS]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), Royal Bank of Canada (RY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RY]), Cheniere Energy (LNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LNG]), Autodesk (ADSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADSK]), PENN Entertainment (PENN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PENN]), Intuit (INTU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTU]), MannKind (MNKD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNKD]), Rocket Companies (RKT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RKT]), Opko Health (OPK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPK]), iQIYI (IQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IQ]), MP Materials (MP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MP]), Sunrun (RUN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RUN]), Contura Energy (CTRA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTRA]), Pembina Pipeline (PBA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PBA]), Clover Health (CLOV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLOV]), Hormel Foods (HRL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HRL]), Coupang (CPNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CPNG]), Ambarella (AMBA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMBA]), NetApp (NTAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTAP]), Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTZ]), and more.

FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 27

The week wraps up on a lighter note, with only a handful of earnings scheduled for Friday. title: C3.ai, Inc. (AI) is Attracting Investor Attention: Here is What You Should Know
content:
C3.ai, Inc. (AI) has been one of the most searched-for stocks on Zacks.com lately. So, you might want to look at some of the facts that could shape the stock's performance in the near term.

Shares of this company have returned -17.5% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -0.8% change. The Zacks Computers - IT Services industry, to which C3.ai belongs, has lost 14.5% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term?

Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision.

Earnings Estimate Revisions

Here at Zacks, we prioritize appraising the change in the projection of a company's future earnings over anything else. That's because we believe the present value of its future stream of earnings is what determines the fair value for its stock.

Our analysis is essentially based on how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to take the latest business trends into account. When earnings estimates for a company go up, the fair value for its stock goes up as well. And when a stock's fair value is higher than its current market price, investors tend to buy the stock, resulting in its price moving upward. Because of this, empirical studies indicate a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and short-term stock price movements.

For the current quarter, C3.ai is expected to post a loss of $0.29 per share, indicating a change of -141.7% from the year-ago quarter. The Zacks Consensus Estimate remained unchanged over the last 30 days.

The consensus earnings estimate of -$1.21 for the current fiscal year indicates a year-over-year change of -195.1%. This estimate has remained unchanged over the last 30 days.

For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $1 indicates a change of +17.6% from what C3.ai is expected to report a year ago. Over the past month, the estimate has changed -2%.

With an impressive externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool -- the Zacks Rank -- is a more conclusive indicator of a stock's near-term price performance, as it effectively harnesses the power of earnings estimate revisions. The size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, has resulted in a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) for C3.ai.

Story Continues

The chart below shows the evolution of the company's forward 12-month consensus EPS estimate:

12 Month EPS12-month consensus EPS estimate for AI

Projected Revenue Growth

Even though a company's earnings growth is arguably the best indicator of its financial health, nothing much happens if it cannot raise its revenues. It's almost impossible for a company to grow its earnings without growing its revenue for long periods. Therefore, knowing a company's potential revenue growth is crucial.

For C3.ai, the consensus sales estimate for the current quarter of $75.82 million indicates a year-over-year change of -23.2%. For the current and next fiscal years, $298.63 million and $331.68 million estimates indicate -23.2% and +11.1% changes, respectively.

Last Reported Results and Surprise History

C3.ai reported revenues of $75.15 million in the last reported quarter, representing a year-over-year change of -20.3%. EPS of -$0.25 for the same period compares with -$0.06 a year ago.

Compared to the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $75.12 million, the reported revenues represent a surprise of +0.04%. The EPS surprise was +21.88%.

The company beat consensus EPS estimates in each of the trailing four quarters. The company topped consensus revenue estimates three times over this period.

Valuation

Without considering a stock's valuation, no investment decision can be efficient. In predicting a stock's future price performance, it's crucial to determine whether its current price correctly reflects the intrinsic value of the underlying business and the company's growth prospects.

Comparing the current value of a company's valuation multiples, such as its price-to-earnings (P/E), price-to-sales (P/S), and price-to-cash flow (P/CF), to its own historical values helps ascertain whether its stock is fairly valued, overvalued, or undervalued, whereas comparing the company relative to its peers on these parameters gives a good sense of how reasonable its stock price is.

As part of the Zacks Style Scores system, the Zacks Value Style Score (which evaluates both traditional and unconventional valuation metrics) organizes stocks into five groups ranging from A to F (A is better than B; B is better than C; and so on), making it helpful in identifying whether a stock is overvalued, rightly valued, or temporarily undervalued.

C3.ai is graded F on this front, indicating that it is trading at a premium to its peers. Click here to see the values of some of the valuation metrics that have driven this grade.

Conclusion

The facts discussed here and much other information on Zacks.com might help determine whether or not it's worthwhile paying attention to the market buzz about C3.ai. However, its Zacks Rank #4 does suggest that it may underperform the broader market in the near term.

title: SoundHound vs. C3.ai: Which AI Stock Is the Better Buy Now?
content:
Artificial intelligence remains one of the most compelling long-term investment themes, but the path to sustained shareholder returns is rarely linear. SoundHound AI SOUN and C3.ai AI are two pure-play AI companies that have captured investor attention with distinct approaches—one focused on conversational and voice AI, the other on enterprise-scale AI applications. Both operate within the Zacks Computers – IT Services industry and are riding the broader AI adoption wave, yet their execution and financial trajectories differ meaningfully.

SoundHound has positioned itself as a leader in voice-enabled, agentic AI across automotive, restaurants, financial services, and IoT. C3.ai, meanwhile, offers a comprehensive enterprise AI platform serving industries such as federal, manufacturing, energy and healthcare. With both stocks under pressure over the past six months and the AI narrative evolving from experimentation to monetization, now is an opportune time to compare their fundamentals.

Let’s dive deep and closely compare the fundamentals of the two stocks to determine which one is a better investment now.

Six-Month Market Performance Snapshot: SOUN vs AI

Both stocks have significantly lagged the broader market. Over the past six months, SoundHound and C3.ai have plunged 39.4% and 37.5%, respectively. This compares unfavorably with the Zacks Computers – IT Services industry, down 18.6%, and stands in sharp contrast to the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector, up 10.5%, and the S&P 500, up 9%.

The magnitude of underperformance suggests investor skepticism toward high-beta, loss-making AI names amid a more selective market environment.

6-Month Price PerformanceZacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Valuation Perspective: Premium vs. Discount

On a forward 12-month price-to-sales basis, SoundHound trades at 13.26X, roughly in line with the industry average of 13.29X. C3.ai trades at 4.59X, reflecting a steep discount to peers.

The valuation gap reflects divergent growth profiles. SoundHound is growing revenue at a significantly faster clip, while C3.ai faces near-term revenue contraction. In that context, SoundHound’s premium appears growth-driven, whereas C3.ai’s discount reflects investor caution about slowing demand and persistent losses.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

The Case for SoundHound Stock

SoundHound’s most recent reported quarter underscores its growth momentum. In the third quarter of 2025, the company delivered record revenues of $42 million, up 68% year over year. Management also raised its full-year outlook and highlighted $269 million in cash, giving it financial flexibility to invest in product development and expansion.

On the earnings call, management emphasized that enterprise AI adoption is accelerating and that SoundHound’s deployments now span millions of endpoints across diversified industries. The company’s Polaris speech foundation model and Amelia 7 agentic AI platform are central to its strategy, enabling real-time, multimodal and multi-agent orchestration capabilities. This technical depth is reinforced in its investor materials, which highlight a $140 billion-plus total addressable market and a fully integrated, proprietary voice stack across 25 languages.

Strategically, SoundHound is expanding voice commerce into vehicles and connected devices. At CES 2026, it unveiled agentic AI capabilities that allow drivers to order food, make reservations, pay for parking and book tickets using voice. Partnerships with Parkopedia for in-vehicle parking payments, OpenTable for reservations and TomTom for multi-agent navigation illustrate a clear monetization path around transactional voice AI.

However, despite strong top-line growth, SoundHound continues to post losses, and the Zacks Consensus Estimate for 2026 loss per share is widening slightly to 6 cents from 5 cents over the past 60 days. That said, the projected 2026 loss is still narrower than the prior year’s expected 13-cent loss, and revenues are expected to grow 38.3% in 2026. Execution, cost discipline and scaling margins will be critical to justify its premium valuation.

Story Continues

The Case for C3.ai Stock

C3.ai offers a different AI exposure, centered on enterprise and government-grade applications. In the fiscal second quarter of 2026, the company reported total revenues of $75.1 million, with subscription revenue accounting for $70.2 million, or 93% of total revenue. Subscription-heavy revenue provides greater visibility and recurring revenue quality.

The quarter also showed improving commercial traction. Bookings increased 49% sequentially, and federal bookings grew 89% year over year. Management highlighted strong federal momentum, with total bookings across federal, defense and aerospace up 89% year over year and representing 45% of total bookings. This underscores C3.ai’s positioning as a beneficiary of the shift toward commercial off-the-shelf AI solutions in government agencies.

Partner leverage is another key strength. In the fiscal second quarter, 89% of total bookings were driven through the partner ecosystem, including Microsoft and AWS alliances that are expanding pipeline and joint deal activity. The company also launched C3 AI Agentic Process Automation, aiming to embed AI agents into enterprise workflows.

Still, challenges are evident. Revenue growth remains uneven, and guidance for fiscal 2026 implies year-over-year revenue contraction at the midpoint. Profitability is also distant, with non-GAAP operating losses guided in the $180.5–$210.5 million range for fiscal 2026. The Zacks Consensus Estimate now project fiscal 2026 loss of $1.21 per share, slightly wider than 60 days ago, and a 23.2% decline in revenues. These trends raise concerns about execution consistency despite strong federal traction.

In short, SoundHound’s revisions suggest short-term margin pressure amid expansion, while C3.ai’s revisions point to a tougher demand environment.

Which AI Stock Has Better Upside?

Both companies operate in promising AI segments, and both remain unprofitable. However, the direction of growth matters. SoundHound is demonstrating strong revenue acceleration, expanding partnerships across automotive and voice commerce, and narrowing expected losses year over year. C3.ai, a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) company, offers federal momentum and a strong cash balance, but faces revenue contraction and deeper projected losses. 

You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

Given SoundHound’s higher growth trajectory, expanding monetization ecosystem and relatively stable industry-aligned valuation, it appears to hold better upside potential at this stage. For seeking exposure to scalable, agentic AI platforms with tangible revenue growth, SoundHound, which carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), currently stands out as the more compelling albeit still high-risk AI play.

title: Epam (EPAM) Surpasses Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
content:
Epam (EPAM) came out with quarterly earnings of $3.26 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $3.16 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.84 per share a year ago. These figures are adjusted for non-recurring items.

This quarterly report represents an earnings surprise of +3.17%. A quarter ago, it was expected that this information technology services provider would post earnings of $3.02 per share when it actually produced earnings of $3.08, delivering a surprise of +1.99%.

Over the last four quarters, the company has surpassed consensus EPS estimates four times.

Epam, which belongs to the Zacks Computers - IT Services industry, posted revenues of $1.41 billion for the quarter ended December 2025, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 1.08%. This compares to year-ago revenues of $1.25 billion. The company has topped consensus revenue estimates four times over the last four quarters.

The sustainability of the stock's immediate price movement based on the recently-released numbers and future earnings expectations will mostly depend on management's commentary on the earnings call.

Epam shares have lost about 18.2% since the beginning of the year versus the S&P 500's gain of 0.5%.

What's Next for Epam?

While Epam has underperformed the market so far this year, the question that comes to investors' minds is: what's next for the stock?

There are no easy answers to this key question, but one reliable measure that can help investors address this is the company's earnings outlook. Not only does this include current consensus earnings expectations for the coming quarter(s), but also how these expectations have changed lately.

Empirical research shows a strong correlation between near-term stock movements and trends in earnings estimate revisions. Investors can track such revisions by themselves or rely on a tried-and-tested rating tool like the Zacks Rank, which has an impressive track record of harnessing the power of earnings estimate revisions.

Ahead of this earnings release, the estimate revisions trend for Epam was mixed. While the magnitude and direction of estimate revisions could change following the company's just-released earnings report, the current status translates into a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) for the stock. So, the shares are expected to perform in line with the market in the near future. You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

It will be interesting to see how estimates for the coming quarters and the current fiscal year change in the days ahead. The current consensus EPS estimate is $2.80 on $1.4 billion in revenues for the coming quarter and $12.56 on $5.81 billion in revenues for the current fiscal year.

Story Continues

Investors should be mindful of the fact that the outlook for the industry can have a material impact on the performance of the stock as well. title: Earnings Preview: C3.ai, Inc. (AI) Q3 Earnings Expected to Decline
content:
Wall Street expects a year-over-year decline in earnings on lower revenues when C3.ai, Inc. (AI) reports results for the quarter ended January 2026. While this widely-known consensus outlook is important in gauging the company's earnings picture, a powerful factor that could impact its near-term stock price is how the actual results compare to these estimates.

The earnings report, which is expected to be released on February 25, might help the stock move higher if these key numbers are better than expectations. On the other hand, if they miss, the stock may move lower.

While management's discussion of business conditions on the earnings call will mostly determine the sustainability of the immediate price change and future earnings expectations, it's worth having a handicapping insight into the odds of a positive EPS surprise.

Zacks Consensus Estimate

This company is expected to post quarterly loss of $0.29 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of -141.7%.

Revenues are expected to be $75.82 million, down 23.2% from the year-ago quarter.

Estimate Revisions Trend

The consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has remained unchanged over the last 30 days. This is essentially a reflection of how the covering analysts have collectively reassessed their initial estimates over this period.

Investors should keep in mind that an aggregate change may not always reflect the direction of estimate revisions by each of the covering analysts.

Price, Consensus and EPS SurprisePrice, Consensus and EPS Surprise Chart for AI

Earnings Whisper

Estimate revisions ahead of a company's earnings release offer clues to the business conditions for the period whose results are coming out. Our proprietary surprise prediction model -- the Zacks Earnings ESP (Expected Surprise Prediction) -- has this insight at its core.

The Zacks Earnings ESP compares the Most Accurate Estimate to the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the quarter; the Most Accurate Estimate is a more recent version of the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate. The idea here is that analysts revising their estimates right before an earnings release have the latest information, which could potentially be more accurate than what they and others contributing to the consensus had predicted earlier.

Thus, a positive or negative Earnings ESP reading theoretically indicates the likely deviation of the actual earnings from the consensus estimate. However, the model's predictive power is significant for positive ESP readings only.

A positive Earnings ESP is a strong predictor of an earnings beat, particularly when combined with a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), 2 (Buy) or 3 (Hold). Our research shows that stocks with this combination produce a positive surprise nearly 70% of the time, and a solid Zacks Rank actually increases the predictive power of Earnings ESP.

Story Continues

Please note that a negative Earnings ESP reading is not indicative of an earnings miss. Our research shows that it is difficult to predict an earnings beat with any degree of confidence for stocks with negative Earnings ESP readings and/or Zacks Rank of 4 (Sell) or 5 (Strong Sell).

How Have the Numbers Shaped Up for C3.ai?

For C3.ai, the Most Accurate Estimate is lower than the Zacks Consensus Estimate, suggesting that analysts have recently become bearish on the company's earnings prospects. This has resulted in an Earnings ESP of -9.40%.

On the other hand, the stock currently carries a Zacks Rank of #4.

So, this combination makes it difficult to conclusively predict that C3.ai will beat the consensus EPS estimate.

Does Earnings Surprise History Hold Any Clue?

Analysts often consider to what extent a company has been able to match consensus estimates in the past while calculating their estimates for its future earnings. So, it's worth taking a look at the surprise history for gauging its influence on the upcoming number.

For the last reported quarter, it was expected that C3.ai would post a loss of$0.32 per share when it actually produced a loss of -$0.25, delivering a surprise of +21.88%.

Over the last four quarters, the company has beaten consensus EPS estimates four times.

Bottom Line

An earnings beat or miss may not be the sole basis for a stock moving higher or lower. Many stocks end up losing ground despite an earnings beat due to other factors that disappoint investors. Similarly, unforeseen catalysts help a number of stocks gain despite an earnings miss.

That said, betting on stocks that are expected to beat earnings expectations does increase the odds of success. This is why it's worth checking a company's Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank ahead of its quarterly release. Make sure to utilize our Earnings ESP Filter to uncover the best stocks to buy or sell before they've reported.

C3.ai doesn't appear a compelling earnings-beat candidate. However, investors should pay attention to other factors too for betting on this stock or staying away from it ahead of its earnings release.

Expected Results of an Industry Player

CoStar Group (CSGP), another stock in the Zacks Computers - IT Services industry, is expected to report earnings per share of $0.27 for the quarter ended January 2026. This estimate points to a year-over-year change of +3.9%. Revenues for the quarter are expected to be $891.08 million, up 25.6% from the year-ago quarter.

The consensus EPS estimate for CoStar has remained unchanged over the last 30 days. However, a lower Most Accurate Estimate has resulted in an Earnings ESP of -4.59%.

When combined with a Zacks Rank of #5 (Strong Sell), this Earnings ESP makes it difficult to conclusively predict that CoStar will beat the consensus EPS estimate. The company beat consensus EPS estimates in each of the trailing four quarters.

title: Autodesk, Datadog, Atlassian, C3.ai, and BlackLine Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
content:
What Happened?

A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after investor fears over artificial intelligence disrupting the software industry sparked a broad sell-off.

The anxiety stemmed from the rapid adoption of new 'agentic AI' tools, which some investors believed could dismantle traditional Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) business models. This 'AI Panic' led to indiscriminate selling across the sector. The market move reflected growing concerns about the downside of the AI boom for established software companies.

The stock market overreacts to news, and big price drops can present good opportunities to buy high-quality stocks.

Among others, the following stocks were impacted:

Design Software company Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) fell 2.7%. Is now the time to buy Autodesk? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Cloud Monitoring company Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) fell 2.6%. Is now the time to buy Datadog? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Project Management Software company Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) fell 3%. Is now the time to buy Atlassian? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Data Infrastructure company C3.ai (NYSE:AI) fell 3.3%. Is now the time to buy C3.ai? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Tax Software company BlackLine (NASDAQ:BL) fell 2.7%. Is now the time to buy BlackLine? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free.

Zooming In On C3.ai (AI)

C3.ai’s shares are extremely volatile and have had 35 moves greater than 5% over the last year. In that context, today’s move indicates the market considers this news meaningful but not something that would fundamentally change its perception of the business.

The previous big move we wrote about was 12 days ago when the stock dropped 8.3% on the news that the "AI replacement" narrative reached a fever pitch following the release of new models from Anthropic and OpenAI. The simultaneous debut of Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 and OpenAI's "Frontier" agent platform raised concerns that autonomous agents are no longer just tools, but new operating systems that can cannibalize traditional software. This suggests that specialized applications might be reduced to mere features within frontier models, rendering legacy seat-based licensing models increasingly obsolete. The catalyst is the models' unprecedented agentic power. Opus 4.6’s "software hunting" capability allows it to autonomously audit and patch complex codebases, while OpenAI's Frontier platform bypasses traditional CRM and ticketing interfaces to perform enterprise work directly. By commoditizing sophisticated workflows into low-cost API calls, these releases threaten the recurring revenue of software giants. As AI builds bespoke tools on demand, the market is aggressively repricing the entire software application layer.

Story Continues

C3.ai is down 24.3% since the beginning of the year, and at $10.40 per share, it is trading 67.3% below its 52-week high of $31.78 from February 2025. Investors who bought $1,000 worth of C3.ai’s shares 5 years ago would now be looking at an investment worth $74.28.

title: C3.ai Vonage Alliance Puts Agentic AI Directly In Field Operations
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

C3.ai (NYSE:AI) has partnered with Vonage to introduce an AI powered field services module focused on real time support for mobile technicians. The new offering combines C3.ai’s enterprise AI tools with Vonage’s communications and network APIs to support on site issue resolution and compliance workflows. The collaboration targets industries with large field workforces, aiming to reduce delays, improve data access in the field, and streamline service operations.

C3.ai is moving to deepen its role in enterprise AI by tying its software more closely to front line operations, not just back office analytics. The company’s shares recently traded at $10.38, with returns of 2.3% over the past week and a 23.5% decline over the past month. Over longer periods, returns have been weak, including a 24.5% decline year to date and a 68.5% decline over the past year.

For investors tracking NYSE:AI, this Vonage collaboration highlights C3.ai’s push into agentic AI tools that sit directly in the hands of technicians. The key question now is how effectively the new module gains adoption among large mobile workforces and whether it can help shift sentiment after multi year share price declines of 57.2% over three years and 92.2% over five years.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for C3.ai by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on C3.ai.NYSE:AI Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Feb 2026

0 things going right for C3.ai that this headline doesn't cover.

This collaboration plugs C3.ai’s agentic AI platform directly into day to day field work, not just central dashboards. By tying C3 AI Field Services to Vonage’s voice, video and Quality on Demand network APIs, the company is looking to make its software part of how technicians diagnose issues, access documentation and bring in remote experts. That positions C3.ai more squarely against enterprise players like ServiceNow, Salesforce and Microsoft that already support field service workflows, but with a stronger focus on AI driven decision support and real time guidance in harsh environments where connectivity is a constraint.

How This Fits Into The C3.ai Narrative

The focus on mobile first AI agents and production deployments in complex industries lines up with the narrative that broader enterprise adoption across manufacturing, chemicals and government could support longer term platform usage. The need to prove commercial traction for a new module and convert pilots into recurring contracts speaks to the concerns around sales execution issues and partner dependency that have been highlighted as key uncertainties. The tighter integration with a telecom owned provider like Vonage, including 5G oriented network APIs, adds a new distribution and product angle that is not fully reflected in the existing focus on hyperscaler and systems integrator partnerships.

Story Continues

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story.  Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for C3.ai to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ C3.ai remains unprofitable and is not forecast to reach profitability in the next 3 years, so any new partnership still needs to translate into improved unit economics and clearer earnings visibility. ⚠️ The company has seen significant insider selling over the past 3 months, which may cause some investors to question management’s conviction while the business is still working through execution and margin pressures. 🎁 If C3 AI Field Services gains traction, it could deepen C3.ai’s role in mission critical operations, supporting stickier customer relationships and potentially better retention across large industrial and government clients. 🎁 Working with Vonage’s communications and network APIs gives C3.ai a differentiated angle in field operations compared with generalist AI platforms offered by larger software peers.

What To Watch Going Forward

You may want to watch for evidence that customers are moving beyond initial trials into broad rollouts of C3 AI Field Services, as well as any disclosures on bookings tied specifically to field service use cases. Commentary on the upcoming earnings call about partner sourced pipeline, especially with Vonage and other telecom or cloud partners, could help you judge whether this kind of alliance is easing past sales execution issues. It may also be useful to monitor how competitors such as ServiceNow, Salesforce and Microsoft talk about AI powered field service to see whether C3.ai is carving out a clear niche or facing heavy pricing and feature pressure.

title: C3.ai Broadens Partner Strategy: Can Telecom Accelerate Revenues?
content:
C3.ai’s AI partnership with Vonage, part of Ericsson (“ERIC”), to develop a network-enabled, agentic AI field-services solution highlights the company’s broader strategy of scaling growth through its partner ecosystem and targeted, industry-specific applications. The collaboration introduces C3 AI Field Services, a module within the C3 AI Asset Performance Suite, designed for mission-critical operations performed beyond the enterprise edge, where workers rely on reliable connectivity and real-time intelligence in remote or demanding environments.

The joint solution addresses persistent challenges in the global field-service market, including complex equipment maintenance, fragmented data systems, workforce skill gaps and delayed troubleshooting. By integrating C3.ai’s enterprise AI capabilities with Vonage’s communications and network APIs, the platform enables mobile AI agents to deliver real-time guidance, troubleshooting support, and voice- and video-based collaboration with remote experts. These capabilities are intended to improve first-time fix rates, safety compliance and workforce productivity, while reducing downtime across distributed operations.

The announcement also reinforces the central role of partnerships in C3.ai’s go-to-market strategy. During the second-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings call, management noted that 89% of quarterly bookings were closed with or through partners, underscoring how ecosystem relationships are accelerating adoption. The company emphasized that rapidly demonstrating economic value remains a key priority for driving faster sales conversion, and collaborations such as the Vonage partnership — supported by Ericsson’s broader telecom ecosystem — could expand distribution channels and help translate enterprise AI deployments into more scalable and recurring revenue growth over time.

Overall, the Vonage partnership reinforces C3.ai’s partner-led strategy and expands AI into telecom-enabled field operations. If adoption scales, such integrations could meaningfully support recurring revenue growth and improve long-term visibility.

Competitive Landscape for C3.AI

Unisys Corporation UIS is targeting the opportunity through enterprise workflow automation, with a focus on deploying agentic AI across IT services and support functions. Its Service Experience Accelerator combines generative and agentic AI with knowledge management to automate service desk operations, preserve institutional knowledge and enhance response times. The company is prioritizing domain-specific, mission-critical use cases where dependable AI agents can drive cost savings and operational efficiency.

Snowflake Inc. SNOW is a cloud-based data platform used for storing, managing, and analyzing large amounts of data, enabling organizations to power analytics, build data applications, and perform AI/ML workloads in a unified environment. AI capabilities are increasingly shaping bookings and customer engagement, with revenue growth driven primarily by sustained usage across existing workloads rather than one-time conversion events. Strong retention rates and expanding AI adoption continue to support this consumption-based model, although revenue realization remains closely linked to customer utilization patterns.

Story Continues

content:
For Immediate Release

Chicago, IL – February 12, 2026 – Zacks.com announces the list of stocks featured in the Analyst Blog. Every day the Zacks Equity Research analysts discuss the latest news and events impacting stocks and the financial markets. Stocks recently featured in the blog include: Palantir Technologies Inc. PLTR, Snowflake Inc. SNOW and C3.ai, Inc. AI.

Here are highlights from Wednesday’s Analyst Blog:

This AI Defense Stock Is One That Wall Street Can't Stop Buying

In the enterprise data and AI analytics space, Palantir Technologies Inc. stands out from peers, such as Snowflake Inc. and C3.ai,Inc., because of its highly scalable, artificial intelligence (AI)-driven platform, robust revenues and profit margins, and reliable cash flows. This positions the AI-focused defense stock as a compelling buy. Let's see in detail –

Palantir's AIP Drives Explosive Growth and Profit Momentum

Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform ("AIP") enables organizations to seamlessly integrate and deploy AI and large language models across highly complex data infrastructures. Consequently, AIP has gained immense popularity among both U.S. commercial clients and the government.

Strong demand for AIP helped Palantir's fourth-quarter 2025 revenues of the U.S. commercial client segment to hit $507 million, up 137% year over year and 28% quarter over quarter, according to investors.palantir.com. In the same period, revenues for the government segment soared 66% year over year and 17% sequentially to $570 million.

The growing adoption of AIP has led Palantir to forecast full-year 2026 revenues of $7.182-$7.198 billion, more than double the $3.320 billion reported in 2025. Growth momentum also appears sustainable, as the remaining deal value of U.S. commercial reached $4.38 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025, up 145% year over year and 21% sequentially.

Palantir projects steady improvement in its GAAP operating income and net income across every quarter of 2026. With a Rule of 40 score of 127%, well above the 40% benchmark, the company demonstrates a highly scalable business model that supports its growth outlook. Palantir also has a strong competitive moat. Its two flagship platforms, Gotham and Foundry, don't face meaningful competition, which underpins predictable cash flows over the long run.

Palantir Stock to Buy Hand Over Fist

While unprofitable projects, reduced AI investments, or potential defense budget cuts could weigh on Palantir's growth trajectory, such risks appear distant. Led by Alex C. Karp, Palantir is well-positioned to gain from the growing adoption of AIP, its scalable business model, and its strong competitive advantage.

Story Continues

Palantir's net profit margin of 36.3%, more than the Internet - Software industry's 26.3%, further indicates significant growth potential.

Brokers are also bullish on Palantir's growth prospects. They estimate an average short-term price target for PLTR stock at $201.38, a 40.9% increase from the last closing price of $142.91. The highest target is $260, suggesting a potential upside of 81.9%.

As a result, Palantir has become a Wall Street favorite. Its Zacks Consensus Estimate for earnings per share (EPS) of $1.34 implies 100% year-over-year growth. Currently, Palantir holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.
